feedback.text: As of current situation, many video game publishers are selling their products (digitally-purchased video games) to the customers without guaranteed of end-of-life plan. A customer purchasing a video game should have ability to play an already bought product. This is often not the case, as publishers can remotely (and at any time) disable the product, leaving the customer without ability to play. This has already happened many times (ex. "The Crew" published by Ubisoft), but many games are at risk. All I (and others) want is for publisher to have and end-of-life plan for a video game, so that a customer can still enjoy purchased product indefinitely, even after publisher decides to no longer support it. This doesn't mean we seek ownership, intellectual and/or monetization rights to the product - only that the publisher will leave the product in a playable state. I have mentioned that others want this too, because as of this day (30/07/2025) the European Citizens' Initiative "Stop Destroying Videogames" has acquired over 1.4 million signatures. All we want is some basic consumer rights to enjoy products that we legally purchased (as it is the case with other products).
